message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-009 in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
What: run_study persists the provenance trail as a JSON artifact and correlates it to the MLflow run (tracker.log_artifact).
Test(s): test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort (asserts _provenance.json artifact)
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commit 2eea9584d8151b0893d19cb580c69508492870a9
Author: Thomas Jones <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Tue Jun 16 23:56:24 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p4): batch C — provenance, observability & resample memory (G-009,010,012,023)
    
    Pass-4 confirmed findings (2-agent verified, revert-discriminated where applicable):
    
    - G-009 (medium) run_study now persists the hash-chained provenance trail as a JSON
      artifact and correlates it to the MLflow run (tracker.log_artifact), so an auditor
      opening a run can retrieve the provenance behind its metrics. Best-effort, like the
      other tracker calls.
    - G-010 (medium) the numpy resample-stability path uses searchsorted on a sorted null
      (O((R+N) log N) time, O(R) memory) instead of an R×N boolean matrix that peaked at
      ~250MB for R=5000,N=50000 and could OOM. Integer counts are identical (the 300-case
      numpy==pure equivalence test still passes).
    - G-012 (medium) from_records raises the typed ProvenanceError (not a raw KeyError) on a
      record missing a required field, so all trail-deserialization failures surface as one
      catchable error.
    - G-023 (low) the corpus unit-id set is computed once and reused for the split-id and
      feature-id validations (was built twice on the same corpus).
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 509 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

690	        # ---- REPORT ----
691	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
692	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
693	            trail.record(
694	                "report",
695	                "assembled reproducibility package",
696	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
697	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
698	            )
699	
700	        if tuple(visited) != required:
701	            raise FirewallViolation(
702	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
703	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
704	            )
705	        if verify_trail:
706	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
707	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
708	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
709	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
710	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
711	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
712	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
713	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
714	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
715	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
716	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
717	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
718	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
719	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
720	        trail.record(
721	            "run_end",
722	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
723	            study=defn.name,
724	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
725	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
726	        )
727	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
728	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
729	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
730	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
731	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
732	
733	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
734	                import json
735	                import tempfile
736	
737	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
738	
739	                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
740	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
741	                ) as fh:
742	                    json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
743	                    path = fh.name
744	                t.log_artifact(rid, path)
745	
746	            track(_log_trail)
747	        ok = True
748	    except Exception as exc:
749	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
750	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
751	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
752	        try:
753	            trail.record(
754	                "run_failed",
755	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
756	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
757	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
758	                last_phase=last_phase,
759	            )
760	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
761	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
762	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
763	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
764	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
765	            _log.warning(
766	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
767	                type(inner).__name__,
768	                inner,
769	            )

content.content:
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343

345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),

211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	    """
219	    return tuple(
220	        {
221	            "seq": e.seq,
222	            "kind": e.kind,
223	            "summary": e.summary,
224	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
225	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
226	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
227	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
228	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
229	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
230	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
231	        }
232	        for e in entries
233	    )
234	
235	
236	def verify_records(
237	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
238	    *,
239	    secret: bytes | None = None,
240	) -> None:

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cp /tmp/pipeline_bak.py src/assay_engine/pipeline.py && git diff --quiet src/assay_engine/pipeline.py && echo "RESTORED CLEAN"

content.text:
The note refers to my own restore (no diff now). The file is clean. The truncated content is just the file header. My verification is complete.

Findings:

The G-009 fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` (lines 728-746) is correct and complete:

- After the terminal `run_end` provenance entry and the `run_duration_s` SLO metric, `run_study` registers `_log_trail`, which serializes the full hash-chained trail via `entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))` to a temp file with suffix `_provenance.json` and calls `tracker.log_artifact(rid, path)`.
- It is routed through the existing `track()` helper (line 317), so any tracker failure is caught and recorded as a `tracking_error` provenance entry rather than aborting the run — consistent with the "best-effort" contract for all other tracker calls, and idiomatic with the surrounding code.
- It is guarded by `tracker is not None and run_id is not None` (track() also re-checks), so it's a no-op when no tracker is wired.
- Uses `entries_to_records` which deep-thaws nested FrozenDicts, so the JSON dump won't choke on FrozenDict payloads — appropriate choice.

Test discrimination verified empirically: `test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort` passes with the fix in place. I reverted only the artifact-logging block and re-ran the test — it failed precisely at line 454 (`assert artifacts and any(str(c[1]).endswith("_provenance.json") for c in artifacts)`) with `assert ([])`. I then restored the file to clean. The test genuinely discriminates against a revert.

No regression: the change is purely additive at end-of-success path; the failure path (BoomTracker) sub-assertion still holds because tracker errors are absorbed into `tracking_error` entries.
